channel.title: Language on earmuff
channel.description: Recent content in Language on earmuff

item.title: Bars and the step grid
item.description:
Bars and the step grid#

A bar lays events on a grid. The grid’s step is the bar’s duration, and the cursor advances exactly one step per token.

bar quarter { C E G _ } // four quarter-note slots
bar 8 { C C C C C C C C } // an eighth-note grid: eight slots

A duration can be a word (whole, half, quarter) or a number (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128), with the suffix . for dotted (×1.5) and t for triplet (×2/3).

item.title: Notes and chords
item.description:
Notes and chords#

Notes and chords are written by name, directly in musical position — the note/chord keywords are optional.

Notes#

A note is a letter A–G, an optional accidental (# or b), and an optional octave number:

C C# Eb F#3 Cb

Without an octave, a default register is used; an explicit number (C5, F#3, E2) pins the octave.

Chords#

Chords are written by name:

Am7 Gmaj7 C7 Dm7b5 C7/E

Slash chords (C7/E) specify the bass note.

item.title: Patterns and control flow
item.description:
Patterns and control flow#

Patterns and control flow are evaluated at compile time. There is no runtime branching: for, if, let, and pattern calls all run during elaboration over compile-time-known values, so the event stream is fully determined before any MIDI is emitted. Loops are bounded and bindings are immutable, which guarantees termination and keeps the music deterministic and diffable.

Patterns#

A pattern is a named, reusable block. It can take parameters and is invoked with a call:

item.title: MIDI and dynamics
item.description:
MIDI and dynamics#

earmuff exposes the full MIDI set as first-class statements, alongside notes and chords.

Raw events#

program "violin"; // program change (by name or number)
cc 74 = 64; // control change
bend +2; // pitch bend, in semitones
pressure 90; // channel aftertouch
sysex F0 7E 7F 09 01 F7; // raw system-exclusive bytes

These can sit in a step slot or be placed with on beat.

Bend#

bend is expressed in semitones. bend +2 bends two semitones up; the elaborator emits the RPN pitch-bend-range message once per track so the result is correct regardless of the synth’s default range, then the 14-bit value for the requested offset. Escape hatches:
